simple_design: URL Queue Manager (FIFO queue for URLs to visit)
complex_design: Distributed Worker Pool (Scalable fetching and parsing units)
Deduplication Service (Using Bloom Filters or distributed cache to track visited URLs)
Message Queue System (e.g., Kafka) for decoupling fetchers and storage
Politeness/Rate Limiter Module (To respect server load and avoid bans)

Vector Databases Deep Dive for System De.sla: P95 latency under 100ms for vector search queries; sustained QPS of 10,000+ for ingestion and query operations.
simple_design: Vector Indexing Service (using a single database instance)
complex_design: Distributed Vector Index Sharding (e.g., using HNSW index partitions across multiple nodes)
Asynchronous Embedding Generation Pipeline (separate service for high-throughput ingestion)
Tiered Storage Strategy (hot vectors in memory/SSD, cold vectors in object storage)
Re-ranking Service (post-retrieval filtering to improve relevance)
key_questions: How do you choose between Approximate Nearest Neighbor (ANN) algorithms like HNSW and LSH, considering trade-offs between recall and query latency?
Design a system to handle billions of vectors with low-latency retrieval. How would you shard the index and manage data consistency during updates?
Describe the full lifecycle of an embedding: from raw text input to indexed vector storage, focusing on throughput bottlenecks.
